row_mappings.rationale: Constant power, work done, driving force and acceleration under resistance control the question.
row_mappings.evidence_summary: 1 A lorry of mass 16000 kg is travelling along a straight horizontal road. The engine of the lorry is working at constant power. row_mappings.rationale: Resolving the applied force with friction and acceleration uses Newton's second law.
row_mappings.evidence_summary: 2 A particle P of mass 0.4 kg is on a rough horizontal floor. The coefficient of friction between P and the floor is μ. row_mappings.rationale: Kinetic energy, potential energy and work against resistance control the surface-motion task.

row_mappings.topic_title: Kinematics of Motion in a Straight Line
row_mappings.rationale: Constant-acceleration motion over straight-line intervals controls the question.
row_mappings.evidence_summary: 4 A cyclist travels along a straight road with constant acceleration. He passes through points A, B and C. row_mappings.rationale: Coplanar force resolution, resultant direction and equilibrium control the question.
row_mappings.evidence_summary: Coplanar forces, of magnitudes F N, 3 N, 6 N and 4 N, act at a point P, as shown in the diagram. (a) Given that α = 60, and that the resultant of the four forces is in the direction of the 3 N force, find F. (b) Given instead that the four forces are in equilibrium, find the values of F and α.

row_mappings.rationale: The multi-part vehicle problem culminates in a collision mass calculation using conservation of momentum; Newton-law towing/braking subparts support that setup.
row_mappings.evidence_summary: 6 On a straight horizontal test track, driverless vehicles (with no passengers) are being tested. row_mappings.topic_title: Kinematics of Motion in a Straight Line
row_mappings.rationale: Piecewise displacement-time functions, velocity and total distance in straight-line motion control the question.
